description:
You are an edge–computing engineer preparing a small fleet of IoT devices for deployment.  
    A colleague left you a few helper files inside “/home/user/iot/”.  
    Your job is to use *only* standard GNU/Linux utilities—specifically **awk** and **sed**—to
    1) analyse the supplied CSV inventory,  
    2) produce two clean-room report files whose exact syntax is described below, and  
    3) bump the software version inside a tiny INI file while recording the change.

    Existing directory layout that you will find when you start the task:
    /home/user/iot/
        ├── inventory.csv          (device telemetry – comma-separated)
        └── deploy/
            └── config.ini         (simple INI file with a “version=” key)

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    1.  inventory.csv – column specification
        • Field-1 : device_id   (string, no spaces)  
        • Field-2 : location    (string, no spaces)  
        • Field-3 : cpu_temp    (integer, °C)  
        • Field-4 : mem_usage   (integer, percent)  
        • Field-5 : status      (string, one of: ok, error)

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    2.  What you must create
        A) /home/user/iot/reports/critical_devices.log  
           • Directory “/home/user/iot/reports/” does not yet exist – create it.  
           • One line per *critical* device, sorted in ascending lexical order by device_id.  
           • A device is considered critical if **any** of these are true:  
             – cpu_temp  > 75                → flag “HighTemp”  
             – mem_usage > 80                → flag “HighMem”  
             – status    == “error”          → flag “Error”  
           • If more than one condition is triggered, list *all* triggered
             flags in the order HighTemp,HighMem,Error, joined by an ampersand “&”.
           • Exact line format (no extra spaces):  
             device_id|location|flag1[&flag2&flag3]
           • The file must end with a single trailing newline character.

        B) /home/user/iot/reports/summary_metrics.txt  
           • Two lines, exactly in this order:  
               AverageCPUtemp=<value>  
               AverageMemUsage=<value>  
           • <value> must be the arithmetic mean across **all** devices
             in inventory.csv, printed with exactly two digits after the decimal
             point (use POSIX “.” decimal point).  
           • A final newline after the second line is required.

        C) /home/user/iot/deploy/config.ini  
           • It already contains a line `version=1.4.2`.  
             Replace it **in-place** so it reads `version=1.4.3` (nothing else
             in the file may change).

        D) /home/user/iot/deploy/update.log  
           • Append a single new line recording the modification.  
           • Exact format (no brackets, single spaces):  
               Config updated from 1.4.2 to 1.4.3 on YYYY-MM-DD  
           • Substitute YYYY-MM-DD with today’s UTC date as produced by the
             command `date -u +%F`.  The log line **must** terminate with a newline.
           • The file should be created if it does not already exist.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    3.  Constraints
        • Do **not** use python, perl, grep, cut, or other helpers—stick to
          awk, sed, standard POSIX shell utilities (mkdir, echo, etc.).
        • Do not touch any other file or directory outside /home/user/iot/.
        • The final four artefacts listed above will be automatically verified
          by a test-suite, including exact byte-for-byte comparison of their
          contents and presence of trailing newlines, so be meticulous.

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    Deliverables that must exist once you finish:
        /home/user/iot/reports/critical_devices.log
        /home/user/iot/reports/summary_metrics.txt
        /home/user/iot/deploy/config.ini         (with updated version)
        /home/user/iot/deploy/update.log         (with appended entry)

    You now have shell access—proceed to craft the required awk and sed
    pipelines and ensure every output file conforms precisely to the formats
    described above.
truth:
<!-- ------------------------------------------------------------------
         Pre-existing files provided to the agent at container start
         ------------------------------------------------------------------ -->
    File: /home/user/iot/inventory.csv
    Content (with final newline):
device01,edge-lab,68,45,ok
device02,edge-lab,77,50,ok
device03,edge-lab,72,85,ok
device04,field,80,90,error
device05,field,60,40,ok

    File: /home/user/iot/deploy/config.ini
    Content (with final newline):
[deployment]
device_group=edge
version=1.4.2

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    # Expected state AFTER the agent finishes the task
    # ---------------------------------------------------------------

    File: /home/user/iot/reports/critical_devices.log
    Expected content (with final newline):
device02|edge-lab|HighTemp
device03|edge-lab|HighMem
device04|field|HighTemp&HighMem&Error

    File: /home/user/iot/reports/summary_metrics.txt
    Expected content (with final newline):
AverageCPUtemp=71.40
AverageMemUsage=62.00

    File: /home/user/iot/deploy/config.ini
    Expected final content (with final newline):
[deployment]
device_group=edge
version=1.4.3

    File: /home/user/iot/deploy/update.log
    • Must exist.
    • Must contain exactly one line (plus trailing newline) matching:
      ^Config updated from 1\.4\.2 to 1\.4\.3 on [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}\n$
      (i.e., date portion verified but not hard-coded).
